{"step_by_step_thinking": "Cementoma is a benign odontogenic tumor that can be diagnosed using radiographs. Radiographs can show the characteristic features of a cementoma, such as a well-defined radiolucent lesion with a sclerotic margin. Subluxation teeth can be diagnosed using radiographs to show the displacement of the tooth. Acute alveolar abscess can be diagnosed using radiographs to show the periapical radiolucency. Impacted canines can be diagnosed using radiographs to show the position and relationship of the impacted tooth to adjacent structures. Therefore, radiographs are helpful in diagnosing all the mentioned conditions except for cementoma.", "answer_choice": "A"}
